reboot_methods.title: Reboot via Power Cord
reboot_methods.description: Restart the router by disconnecting and reconnecting the power cord (recommended first step)
steps: Disconnect the power cord from the router and the modem.
Wait 10 seconds and reconnect the power cord to the modem. Make sure it has power.
Wait until the modem's online indicator has stopped blinking (about two minutes). Reconnect the power cord to the router.
Wait until the power indicator stops blinking. Wait two minutes before trying to connect to the internet from a computer.

reboot_methods.title: Reboot via Linksys Smart Wi-Fi
reboot_methods.description: Restart the router through the Linksys Smart Wi-Fi admin interface
steps: Log in to Linksys Smart Wi-Fi. (Open a browser and go to http://192.168.1.1 or linksyssmartwifi.com and sign in.)
Click Yes. The router will reboot. While the router is rebooting, all connected devices will lose their internet connection, and will automatically reconnect when the router is ready again.

reboot_methods.title: Factory Reset (Last Resort)
reboot_methods.description: Restore the router to factory default settings — use only when all else fails
reboot_methods.warning: This will erase all custom settings including your Wi-Fi name, password, and any custom configurations. You will need to set up the router again from scratch.
reboot_methods.caution: Only use factory reset if power cord reboot and Smart Wi-Fi reboot have not resolved the issue.
steps: Make sure the router is powered on.
Locate the Reset button on the back panel of the router.
Press and hold the Reset button with a paperclip or pin until the power LED starts flashing (about 10 seconds).
Release the Reset button and wait for the router to finish resetting. The router will reboot automatically.
Once the router has restarted, it will broadcast the default Wi-Fi network name and password printed on the label on the bottom of the router.
Reconnect your devices using the default Wi-Fi credentials from the router label.
Install the router again by opening a web browser and following the on-screen setup instructions.

power.stays_on_steadily: Power is connected and router is operating normally (also indicates successful Wi-Fi Protected Setup connection)
power.flashes_slowly: Router is booting up, a firmware upgrade is in progress, or a Wi-Fi Protected Setup connection is being made
power.flashes_quickly: Wi-Fi Protected Setup error has occurred

hardware.reset_button: Press and hold until the power LED starts flashing to reset the router to factory settings. You can also restore the factory settings using Linksys Smart Wi-Fi.
hardware.power_switch: Press | (on) to turn on the router.
hardware.internet_port: Yellow gigabit port — connect to your broadband cable/DSL/fiber modem.
hardware.ethernet_ports: Blue gigabit ports — connect to wired devices on your network.

support.description: Award-winning customer support, documentation, downloads, FAQs, technical support, live chat, and forums

when_to_reboot: The internet appears to be unavailable (browser shows 'Cannot find [internet address]')
Router is unresponsive or devices cannot connect to the internet
Internet connection is slow or intermittent across multiple devices
Network cables and power are confirmed connected but internet still not working
Router has been running for an extended period without restart
when_not_to_reboot: Issue is specific to one website (likely a server-side issue — check your ISP for outages)
Issue is on only one device (likely device-specific — check that device's Wi-Fi settings)
Firmware update is in progress (power light flashing slowly — wait for it to complete)
ISP has confirmed a service outage in your area (rebooting will not restore their service)
Router was not successfully set up initially (follow setup instructions instead)
internet_unavailable_checklist: Make sure that the network and power cables are securely connected.
Make sure that the power outlet that your router is connected to has power.
Contact your ISP and ask about outages in your area.
